questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The name of the curve described parametrically by the equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo></math> is
questions.solution:
Here, we are having x=t2+t+1, y=t2-t+1,

On subtracting y from x, we get

x-y=t2+t+1-t2-t+1

⇒x-y=2t or t=x - y2,

On putting the value of t, in x, we get

x = x - y22 + x - y2 + 1,

On further simplification, we get

x=x2 - 2xy + y24+x - y2+1,

⇒4x=x2-2xy+y2+2x-2y+4,

Final equation, we get as x2-2xy+y2-2x-2y+4=0,

On comparing, this with the general equation of second degree, i.e., ax2+2hxy+by2+2gx+2fy+c=0, 

we will get a=1, h=-1, b=1, g=-1, f=-1, c=4.

Now, to check for the type of conic, we will check

∆ = abc + 2fgh - af2 - bg2 - ch2  and h2 - ab.

∆=4 -2-1-1-4=-4≠0,

Now, h2-ab=1-1=0.

As, we are having ∆ ≠ 0 and h2-ab=0, which is the condition for a parabola.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the line <math><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> is the directrix of the parabola <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>32</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> and the parabola intersects the circle <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> at two real distinct points, then the absolute value of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
x2-ky+32=0
⇒x2=ky-32k
Put, x=X,y-32k=Y
The equation of directrix is Y+k4=0
i.e. y-32k+k4=0
But, y-2=0 is the directrix.
⇒32k-k4=2
⇒k2+8k-128=0
⇒k=-16 or k=8
For k=8, the parabola is x2=8y-4 which does not intersect the circle.
For k=-16, the parabola is x2=-16y+2 which intersects the circle at two real distinct points.
⇒ Absolute value of k=-16=16
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The intercept of the latusrectum to the parabola $y^{2}=4 a x$ are $b$ and $k$, then $k$ is equal to
questions.solution:
For latusrectum $P Q$.
$$
y\left(t_{1}+t_{2}\right)-2 x-2 a t_{1} t_{2}=0
$$
and $\quad t_{1} t_{2}=-1$
For any point $P(x, y)$, the focal distance is $a+x$. $\therefore \quad b=a+x=a+a t_{1}^{2}=a\left(1+t_{1}^{2}\right) \quad \ldots(\mathrm{i})$
$c=a+x=a+a t_{2}^{2}=a+\frac{a}{t_{1}^{2}} \quad\left(\because t_{1} t_{2}=-1\right)$
$=\frac{a\left(1+t_{1}^{2}\right)}{t_{1}^{2}}$...(ii)
$\frac{b}{c}=t_{1}^{2}$
$\therefore$ From Eq. (i), $b=a\left(1+\frac{b}{c}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \quad b=a+\frac{a b}{c}$
$c=\frac{a b}{b-a}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let a focal chord $12 x+5 y-27=0$ of the parabola $\mathrm{y}^2=\mathrm{kx}$ intersect the parabola at the points $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{P}^{\prime}$. If $\mathrm{S}$ is the focus of this parabola, then $9\left(\mathrm{SP}+\mathrm{SP}^1\right)=$
questions.solution:
Given that equation of parabola $y^2=\mathrm{km}$ then focus is $\left(\frac{k}{4}, 0\right)$ which is lies on focal chord. $\therefore 12 \times \frac{k}{4}+0-27=0 \Rightarrow k=9$, focus s $\left(\frac{9}{4}, 0\right)$
On solving the equation $y^2=9 x$ and $12 x+5 y-27=0$ We get $P(1,3)$ and $P^{\prime}\left(\frac{81}{16}, \frac{-27}{4}\right)$.
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore S P=\sqrt{\left(\frac{9}{4}-1\right)^2+3^2}=\frac{13}{4} \\
& S P^{\prime}=\sqrt{\left(\frac{81}{16}-\frac{9}{4}\right)^2+\left(\frac{27}{4}\right)^2}=\frac{117}{16}
\end{aligned}$
Now, $g\left(S P+S P^{\prime}\right)=\frac{1521}{1}=4 S P . S P^{\prime}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi></math> for which the curve <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>λ</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>24</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> represents a parabola, is
questions.solution:
Given, curve is

7x+52+7y+32=λ24x+3y-242,

⇒7x+572+7y+372=λ242+322×4x+3y-2442+322,

⇒49 x+572+y+372=25λ24x+3y-2442+322,

⇒x+572+y+372=25λ2494x+3y-2442+322,

⇒x+572+y+372=5λ7×4x+3y-2442+32.

The L.H.S. of the above equation represent distance of a point x,y from a point -57,-37 and R.H.S. represent its perpendicular distance from a line 4x+3y-24=0.

Hence, the above equation satisfy the basic definition of a conic section, i.e., PS=ePM, where PS is the distance of a variable point from a fixed point (focus) and PM is its perpendicular distance from a fixed line (directrix) and e is the eccentricity.

And, we know that, for parabola e=1.

∴λ=75, ⇒λ=±75.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2 </mn></math> represents
questions.solution:
Given equation is x2-2xy+y2+3x+2=0

Comparing the above  equation with ax2+2hxy+by2+2gx+2fy+c=0

Here, a=1, b=1, h=-1, g=32, f=0, c=2
Now, Δ=abc+2fgh-af2-bg2-ch2

⇒Δ=112+2032-1--102-1322-2-12⇒Δ=-94
i.e., Δ≠0 and h2-ab=1-1=0

i.e., h2=ab

given equation represents a parabola
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The focal distance of a point on the parabola $y^{2}=16 x$ whose ordinate is twice the abscissa, is
questions.solution:
Given curve is $y^{2}=16 x$. Let the point be $(h, k)$. But $2 h=k$, then $k^{2}=16 h$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow & & 4 h^{2} &=16 h \\
\Rightarrow & & h &=0, h=4 \\
\Rightarrow & & k &=0, k=8
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Points are $(0,0),(4,8)$.
Hence, focal distance are respectively
$$
0+4=4,4+4=8
$$
$(\because$ focal distance $=h-a)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If one end of focal chord of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>x</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, then the length of the focal chord is____units
questions.solution:
Given parabola is y2=8x

By comparing with parabola y2=4ax

a = 2

Parametric point on parabola is at2, 2at = 2t2, 4t = 12, 2

⇒4t =2

t = 12

Length of focal chord will be at+1t2

= 212+22

= 2 ×254

=252
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the line $2 b x+3 c y+4 d=0$ passes through the points of intersection of $y^2=4 a x$ and $x^2=4 a y$, then
questions.solution:
Given parabola, we have
$$
x^2=4 a y \text { and } y^2=4 a x
$$

Intersection point of these two parabolas are $(0,0)$ and $(4 a, 4 a)$.
Given that, $2 b x+3 c y+4 d=0$
passes through point of intersection.
Case I If it passes through $(0,0)$, we obtain
$$
\begin{aligned}
2 b(0)+3 c(0)+4 d & =0 \\
\Rightarrow \quad d & =0
\end{aligned}
$$

Case II If it passes through $(4 a, 4 a)$, we obtain
$$
\begin{aligned}
2 b(4 a)+3 c(4 a)+4(0) & =0 \Rightarrow(2 b+3 c)(4 a)=0 \\
\Rightarrow \quad 2 b+3 c & =0
\end{aligned}
$$

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
$$
d^2+(2 b+3 c)^2=0
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the line <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> bisects two chords of the parabola <math><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi></math> which are passing through the point <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then the length of the latus rectum can be equal to
questions.solution:
Any point on y=x–1 can be taken as h,h–1
Taking h,h-1 as the midpoint of chord, the equation of chord is yh-1-4bx+h2=h-12-4bh
Which passes through the point b,-2b
⇒ -2bh-1-2bb+h=h2-2h+1-4bh
⇒ h2-2h+2b2-2b+1=0
As, above equation has two real roots, hence, 
-22-412b2-2b+1>0
⇒ 2b2-2b<0⇒b<1⇒4b<4
⇒ length of latus rectum is less than 4
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The radius of the circle whose centre is<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mo>(</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo></math> and which cuts the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>x</mi></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>, such that its common chord <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> subtends a right angle at the vertex of the parabola is equal to
questions.solution:
The centre of the circle is -4, 0 and let r be the radius of the circle.

Then, we know that the equation of a circle with centre h, k and radius r is x-h2+y-k2=r2 

Using this the equation of the circle is (x+4)2+y2=r2.

This cuts the parabola at points Ax1, y1 and Bx2, y2

Then, we can find the abscissa of A and B by putting the value y2=8x from the parabola to the circle.

Hence, we get an equation (x+4)2+8x=r2,

 ⇒x2+8x+16+8x=r2

⇒x2+16x+16-r2=0.

The roots of this equation are x1 and x2, hence sum of the roots is x1+x2= -161=-16 and product of roots is x1x2=16-r21=16-r2  ...1

The co-ordinates of A and B are given by y12=8x1 and y22=8x2 respectively.

∴ y1=±22x1 and y2=±22x2.

Since, AB subtends a right angle at the vertex of the parabola.

∴ mOA·mOB=-1

⇒ y1-0x1-0y2-0x2-0=-1

∴ y1x1×y2x2=-1 ⇒x1x2+y1y2=0

But, y1=±22x1 and y2=±22x2

⇒x1x2+±22x1±22x2=0 

⇒x1x2+8x1x2=0

⇒x1x2=0 

On putting the value of x1x2 from equation 1, we get  

16-r2=0  ⇒r=4.

The radius of the circle is equal to the absolute value of the x-co-ordinate of the centre, hence the circle touches the Y-axis at origin.

Hence, it also touches the parabola and will not intersect it at any point, so no such circle exist.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Find the equation to the parabola, whose axis parallel to the $y$-axis and which passes through the points $(0,4),(1,9)$ and $(4,5)$ is
questions.solution:
The equation of parabola parallel to $y$-axis is
$y=A x^2+B x+C$ ...(i)
The point $(0,4),(1,9)$ and $(4,5)$ lies on Eq. (i). Then, $\quad 4=0+0+C \Rightarrow C=4$ ...(ii)
$9=A+B+C$
or $\quad 9=A+B+4 \quad(\because C=4)$
$A+B=5$ ...(iii)
and $\quad 5=16 A+4 B+C \quad(\because C=4)$
or $\quad 5=16 A+4 B+4$
$\therefore \quad 16 A+4 B=1$
$4 A+B=\frac{1}{4}$ ...(iv)
Solving Eqs. (iii) and (iv),
we get $\quad A=\frac{-19}{12}, B=\frac{79}{12}$ ...(v)
Substituting the values of $A, B$ and $C$ from Eqs. (ii) and (v) in Eq. (i), then equation of parabola is
$y=\frac{-19}{12} x^2+\frac{79}{12} x+4$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the focal chord drawn through the point $(1,2)$ to the parabola $y^2=8 x$ meets this parabola in $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ and $\left(x_2, y_2\right)$ then $x_1+x_2=$
questions.solution:
Given parabola is $\mathrm{y}^2=8 \mathrm{x}$ with parametric coordinates $\left(\mathrm{at}_1^2, 2 \mathrm{at}{ }_1\right)$ and $\left(\mathrm{at}_2^2, 2 \mathrm{at}_2\right)$. $\operatorname{Let}\left(\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1\right) \rightarrow\left(a \mathrm{t}_1^2, 2 a t_1\right)$ and $\left(\mathrm{x}_2, \mathrm{y}_2\right) \rightarrow\left(a t_2^2, 2 a t_2\right)$ Equation of line passing through $$ \left(\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1\right) \text { and }\left(\mathrm{x}_2, \mathrm{y}_2\right) \text { is }\left(\mathrm{y}-2 \mathrm{at}_1\right) $$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{2 a t_2-2 a t_1}{a t_2^2-a t_1^2}\left(x-a t_1^2\right) \\ & \left(y-2 a t_1\right)=\frac{2}{\left(t_2+t_1\right)}\left(x-a t_1^2\right)\end{aligned}$ Satisfy $(1,2)$, $$ \begin{aligned} & \left(2-2 a t_1\right)=\frac{2}{\left(t_2+t_1\right)}\left(1-a t_1^2\right) \\ & \left(1-a_1\right)=\frac{1}{\left(t_2+t_1\right)}\left(1-a_1^2\right) \\ & \left(1-a t_1\right)\left(t_2+t_1\right)=\left(1-a t_1^2\right) \\ & \mathrm{t}_2+t_1-\mathrm{at}_1 \mathrm{t}_2-\mathrm{at}_1^2=1-\mathrm{at}_1^2 \end{aligned} $$ Here foci $a=2$.
When satisfy foci coordinates $(a, 0)$ then the required equation gives the relation $t_1 t_2=-1$ From (i) $$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{t}_2+\mathrm{t}_1-2(-1)=1 \\ & \mathrm{t}_2+\mathrm{t}_1+2=1 \end{aligned} $$
$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{x}_1+\mathrm{x}_2=a \mathrm{t}_1^2+a \mathrm{t}_2^2 \\ & =\mathrm{a}\left[\mathrm{t}_1^2+\mathrm{t}_2^2+2 \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2-2 \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2\right] \\ & =\mathrm{a}\left[\left(\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2\right)^2-2 \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2\right] \\ & =2\left[(-1)^2-2(-1)\right] \\ & =[1+2]=2 \times 3=6 \end{aligned} $$ So, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The length of the latusrectum of the parabola \(20\left(x^2+y^2-6 x-2 y+10\right)=(4 x-2 y-5)^2\), is
questions.solution:
Given, equation of parabola is
\(\begin{aligned}
20\left(x^2+y^2-6 x-2 y+10\right) & =(4 x-2 y-5)^2 \\
\Rightarrow \quad\left(x^2+y^2-6 x-2 y+10\right) & =\left(\frac{4 x-2 y-5}{\sqrt{20}}\right)^2 \\
\Rightarrow \quad(x-3)^2+(y-1)^2 & =\left(\frac{4 x-2 y-5}{\sqrt{20}}\right)^2 \quad \ldots (i)
\end{aligned}\)
In Eq. (i), focus is \((3,1)\) and equation of directrix is \(4 x-2 y-5=0\).
So, distance from focus to directrix is \(\frac{|12-2-5|}{\sqrt{20}}\)
\(=\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}=2 a\)
Now, length of latursrectum \(=4 a\)
\(=2(2 a)=2 \frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}=\sqrt{5}\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let $O$ be the origin and $A$ be a point on the curve $y^2=4 x$. Then the locus of the mid point of $O A$ is :
questions.solution:
Since $O$ be the origin and $A$ be the point on the curve $y^2=4 x$.
$\therefore$ Co-ordinates of $O$ and $A$ are $(0,0)$ and $\left(a t^2, 2 a t\right)$ respectively.
$\therefore$ Co-ordinates of mid point of $O A$ are $\left(\frac{0+a t^2}{2}, \frac{0+2 a t}{2}\right)=\left(\frac{a t^2}{2}, a t\right)$
$\because \quad(a t)^2=2\left(\frac{a t^2}{2}\right)$
Thus the locus of required point is $y^2=2 x$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $(-1,-1)$ is the focus and $x+y+4=0$ is the directrix of a parabola, then its vertex is
questions.solution:
Let $P(x, y)$ be any point on the parabola whose focus is $S(-1,-1)$ and directrix is $x+y+4=0$ Let $M$ be foot of perpendicular drawn from $P$ to the directrix. Then,
$$
\begin{array}{lc} 
& S P=P M \Rightarrow(S P)^2=(P M)^2 \\
\Rightarrow(x+1)^2+(y+1)^2=\left(\frac{|x+y+4|}{\sqrt{(1)^2+(1)^2}}\right)^2 \\
\Rightarrow & 2\left(x^2+1+2 x+y^2+1+2 y\right) \\
\Rightarrow & \left(x^2+y^2+16+2 x y+8 y+8 x\right) \\
\Rightarrow & \left.\quad 2 x^2+y^2+2 x+2 y+2\right) \\
& =x^2+y^2+2 x y+8 y+8 x+16 \\
\Rightarrow & x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y-2 x y-12=0 \\
\Rightarrow & \quad(x-y)^2-4 x-4 y-12=0 \\
\Rightarrow & (x-y)^2=-4(x+y+3) \\
\text { Let } & x-y=X, x+y+3=Y \\
\therefore & \quad X^2=-4 Y
\end{array}
$$
On comparing with it $X^2=-4 A Y$, we get
$$
A=1
$$
Since, vertex of Eq. (ii) is $(0,0)$.
$$
\begin{array}{cc}
\therefore & X=0, Y=0 \\
\Rightarrow & x-y=0, x+y+3=0 \\
\Rightarrow & x=-\frac{3}{2}, y=\frac{-3}{2}
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math> is the parameter for one end of a focal chord of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math>, then its length is
questions.solution:
For a focal chord of parabola t1t2=-1,where t1and t2 are parameters of two ends of the focal chord. Since one end is given t , then value of the parameter for the other end of the focal chord is =-1t

∴  The co-ordinates of the end points of the focal chords are at2, 2at and at2,-2at

∴  Length of the focal chord =at2-at22+2at+2at2

=at4+1t4-2+4t2+4t2+8

=at4+1t4+4t2+4t2+6=at2+1t2+22

=at2+1t2+2=at+1t2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For the parabola $y=\frac{h^3}{3} x^2+\frac{h^2}{2} x-h+\frac{3}{4 h^3}$ if the equation of directrix is $y=k$, then $k: h$
questions.solution:
Given, $\quad y=\frac{h^3}{3} x^2+\frac{h^2}{2} x-h+\frac{3}{4 h^3}$
$12 h^3 y=4 h^6 x^2+6 h^5 x-12 h^4+9$
$4 h^6 x^2+6 h^5 x=12 h^3 y+12 h^4-9$
$\left(x^2+\frac{3}{2 h} x\right)=\frac{12 h^4}{4 h^6}\left(y+\frac{12 h^4}{12 h^3}-\frac{3}{14 h^3}\right)$
$\left(x^2+\frac{3}{2 h^2}+\frac{3}{16 h^2}\right)=\frac{3}{h^3}\left(y+h-\frac{3}{4 h^3}\right)+\frac{9}{16 h^2}$
$\left(x+\frac{3}{2 h}\right)^2=\frac{3}{h^3}\left(y+\frac{19 h}{16}-\frac{3}{4 h^3}\right)$
Here, $a=\frac{3}{4 h^3}$
$\therefore$ Equation of directrix is
$y+\frac{19 h}{16}-\frac{3}{4 h^3}=\frac{-3}{4 h^3} \Rightarrow y=-\frac{19 h}{16}$
$\therefore \quad k=-\frac{19 h}{16} \Rightarrow \frac{k}{h}=-\frac{19}{16}$
$\therefore \quad k: h=-19: 16$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The locus of the centre of the circle described on any focal chord of the parabola <math><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math> as the diameter is
questions.solution:
Let, Pat12,2at1 and Qat22,2at2 be the extremities of a focal chord PQ of the parabola y2=4ax. Then, t1t2=-1.
Let, h,k be the coordinates of the centre of the circle described on PQ as diameter. Then,
h=a2t12+t22 and k=at1+t2
⇒2ha=t12+t22 and ka2=t1+t22
⇒2ha=t12+t22 and k2a2=t12+t22+2t1t2
⇒k2a2=2ha-2 ∵t1t2=-1
⇒k2=2ah-a
Hence, the locus of h,k is
y2=2ax-a.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let 1 be the directrix of the parabola $9 y^2+12 y+9 x-14=0$ and $l_1$ be the line passing through the vertex of this parabola and the origin. If $(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ is the point of intersection of $l$ and $l_1$, then $\mathrm{h}+\mathrm{k}=$
questions.solution:
Given: $9 y^2+12 y+9 x-14=0$
$\Rightarrow\left(y+\frac{2}{3}\right)^2=-4 \times \frac{1}{4}(x-2)$
Whose vertex is $\left(-\frac{2}{3}, 2\right)$ Equation of line passing through $\left(-\frac{2}{3}, 2\right)$ and $(0,0)$ :
$(y-0)=\frac{2-0}{-\frac{2}{3}-0}(x-0) \Rightarrow y=-3 x$
Now, the equation of directrix of parabola (i) is
$x=\frac{1}{4}+2 \Rightarrow x=\frac{9}{4}$ ...(iii)
Solving $\mathrm{eq}^{\mathrm{n}}$ (ii) \& (iii), we get
$x=\frac{9}{4}$ and $y=-\frac{27}{4} \quad \therefore h=\frac{9}{4} \& k=-\frac{27}{4}$
So, $h+k=\frac{9}{4}-\frac{27}{4}=\frac{-18}{4}=\frac{-9}{2}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two curves <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math> intersect each other at a point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> in first quadrant. Normals are drawn to the curves at <math><mi>P</mi></math>, If they meet <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo> </mo><mo>&</mo><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi></math>  then mid point of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
The two curves intersect at a, 2a and a,–2a

But P=a, 2a

equation of normal to y2=4ax at a, 2a is:

y –2a=–x –a

and equation of normal to a y2=4 x3 at a, 2a is:

y–2a=–13 x-a

∴ A=3a, 0 and B=7a,0

∴ Mid point of AB=5a,0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the parabola $y^2+2 x+2 y-3=0$ and match the items of List-I with those of the List-II.
The correct match is $A \quad B \quad C \quad D$
questions.solution:
We have, equation of parabola is
$\begin{array}{ll} 
& y^2+2 x+2 y-3=0 \\
\Rightarrow & (y+1)^2-1+2 x-3=0 \\
\Rightarrow & (y+1)^2=4-2 x \Rightarrow(y+1)^2=-2(x-2) \\
\therefore & \text { Vertex }=(2,-1)
\end{array}$
Axis : $y+1=0$
Focus : $(-a+2,-1)=\left(-\frac{1}{2}+2,-1\right)=\left(\frac{3}{2},-1\right)$
Directrix : $x-2=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow x=\frac{5}{2} \Rightarrow 2 x-5=0$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>F</mi><mi>B</mi></math> is a focal chord of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>F</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>F</mi><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn></math>, then the latus-rectum of the parabola is equal to
questions.solution:
Given: FA=4 and FB=5, where FA and FB are segments of focal chord.

Since, we know that harmonic mean of  FA, FB is equal to semi latus rectum.

Therefore, 22a=1AF+1FB

⇒a=209⇒4a=809
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The axis of a parabola is along the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi></math> and the distance of its vertex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></math> and that of its focus <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> lie in first quadrant, then the equation of the parabola in parametric form is
questions.solution:
Distance of vertex from origin =2

Distance of focus from origin =22

Axis is along y=x. The focus and the vertex lie in the first quadrant.

Hence, the coordinates of the focus are 2,2.

The vertex is at 1,1

Hence, the equation of directrix is x+y=0

Take point h,k on the parabola.

By definition of the parabola

h-22+k-22=h+k22

⇒2h2+2k2+16-8h-8k=h2+k2+2hk

⇒h2+k2-8h-8k-2hk+16=0

Substitute h,k to x,y

x2+y2-2xy-8x-8y+16=0

This is the equation of parabola.

x-y2=8x+y-2

So, x=t+12 and y=t-12 satisfies above equation, therefore option A is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Through the vertex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>O</mtext></math> of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math> chords <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>OP</mtext></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>OQ</mtext></math> are drawn such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>OP</mtext></math> is perpendicular to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>OQ</mtext></math>. For each and every possible chords <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>OP</mtext></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>OQ</mtext></math>, the chord <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>PQ</mtext></math> cuts the axis of parabola at a fixed point, whose coordinates are
questions.solution:
Given parabola is y2=4ax.

Any point on the above parabola can be assumed as (at2, 2at).

Let us assume that the coordinates of points P and Q are Pat12, 2at1 and Qat22, 2at2.

Therefore, slope of OP=2at1-0at12-0=2t1

and slope of OQ=2at2-0at22-0=2t2

Since, OP⊥OQ, hence 2t1×2t2=-1

⇒t1t2=-4

Also, the equation of the chord joining P and Q is,

y-2at1=2at2-2at1at22-at12x-at12

⇒yt1+t2=2x+2at1t2

Now, t1t2=-4

The equation of PQ is yt1+t2=2x+2at1t2

∴     yt1+t2=2x-8a

⇒t1+t2y-0-2x-4a=0

The above line always passes through the point of intersection of y=0 and x=4a i.e., the fixed point is (4a, 0).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Through the vertex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>O</mi></math> of a parabola <math><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi mathvariant="italic">ax</mi></math>, chords <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>O</mi><mi>P</mi><mo> </mo><mo>&</mo><mo> </mo><mi>O</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> are drawn at right angles to one another. Then The locus of midpoints of the chord <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> is :
questions.solution:
If x,y be the mid point of PQ, then  2 x = a t 1 2 + t 2 2  &  2 y = 2 a t 1 + t 2
 or   2 x = a [ t 1 + t 2 2 - 2 t 1 t 2 ]
 or 2x=ay2a2-2t1t2  ...(1) & ya=t1+t2  ...(2)

 As we know that condition for the chord PQ to subtend a right angle at the vertex of the parabola is  t 1 t 2 = - 4

 [Since PQ subtends a right angle at vertex O(0,0), OP & OQ are perpendicular i.e.,
m 1 m 2 = - 1 or 2 a t 1 a t 1 2 · 2 a t 2 a t 2 2 = - 1 or t 1 t 2 = - 4 ]
 Now, putting the value of  t 1 t 2  in equation 2

 We get,  2 x = a y 2 a 2 + 8

 or  2 a x = y 2 + 8 a 2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A circle of radius 4 , drawn on a chord of the parabola $y^2=8 x$ as diameter, touches the axis of the parabola. Then, the slope of the chord is
questions.solution:
Given, equation of parabola is
$$
\begin{aligned}
y^2 & =8 x \\
a & =2
\end{aligned}
$$
Let $(h, 4)$ be the coordinate of mid-point of chord. Then, equation of chord is
$$
y-4=m(x-h)
$$
If line (ii) passes through the point $P\left(2 t_1^2, 4 t_1\right)$ and $Q\left(2 t_2^2, 4 t_2\right)$ on parabola Eq. (i), then
$$
y\left(t_1+t_2\right)-2 x-4 t_{t_2}=0
$$
having slope
$$
m=\frac{2}{t_1+t_2}
$$
Since, $(h, 4)$ is the mid-point of $P Q$. Therefore,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 \times 4=4\left(t_1+t_2\right) \\
\Rightarrow & t_1+t_2=2
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, slope of chord $P Q$ is
$$
m=\frac{2}{2}=1 \quad \text { [using Eq. (iv)] }
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math><mi>D</mi></math> is a point on the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>l</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>3,3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is a fixed point. The line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>l</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is perpendicular to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi><mi>S</mi></math> and passes through <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> is another point on the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>l</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> (other than <math><mi>D</mi></math> ), then the locus of the point of intersection of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>l</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and the angle bisector of the angle <math><mi>M</mi><mi>D</mi><mi>S</mi></math> is
options: <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>
questions.solution:
Let the point of intersection of l2 and the angle bisector of angle MDS is Ph,k.

Draw perpendicular PF to l1 from P.

Now, ∆PFD & ∆PSD are congruent triangles.

Hence, PF=PS

h+k-22=h-32+k-32
⇒x+y-22=2x-32+y-32
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The locus of the middle points of chords of a parabola which subtend a right angle at the vertex of the parabola, is
questions.solution:
α=ar12+at222; β=2at1+2at22=af2+t2

Also, 2at1-0at12-0×2at2-0at22-0=-1

⇒ t1t2=-4

Now, 2αa=t12+t22=t1+t22-2t1t2

=βa2-2·-4

So, the locus of (α,β) is 2xa=y2a2+8 which is a parabola.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $A(-2,3)$ is a fixed point outside the parabola $y^2=4 a x(a>0)$ and $P$ is a point moving on the parabola. The locus of point $Q$ which divides $A P$ in the ratio $3: 2$ is a conic. Then focus of that conic is
questions.solution:
Let $P$ be $\left(a t^2, 2 a t\right)$ and $Q$ be $(h, k)$. Also given, $A=(-2,3)$ Now, since $Q$ divides $A P$ in the ratio $3: 2$ i.e., $$ \frac{A Q}{Q P}=\frac{3}{2} $$
$$ \begin{array}{rlrl} \Rightarrow & \quad 5 k & =6 a t+6 \\ \Rightarrow \quad t & =\frac{5 k-6}{6 a} \text { put in Eq. (i), we get } \\ 5 h & =3 a\left(\frac{5 k-6}{6 a}\right)^2-4 \\ \Rightarrow \quad 5 h+4 & =\frac{(5 k-6)^2}{12 a} \Rightarrow(5 k-6)^2=60 a h+48 a \\ \Rightarrow \quad(5 k-6)^2 & =12 a(5 h+4) \end{array} $$ Hence, the locus of $Q(h, k)$ is
On comparing Eq. (iii) by $Y^2=4 A X$, we get, $$ \begin{aligned} \quad & Y & =5 y-6, X=5 x+4 \text { and } 4 A=12 a \\ & A & =3 a \end{aligned} $$ So, focus of $Y^2=4 A X$ is $(A, 0)$ i.e. $X=A$ and $Y=0$ Hence, focus of Eq. (iii) is $$ \begin{aligned} & & 5 x+4 & =3 a \text { and } 5 y-6=0 \\ \Rightarrow & & x & =\frac{3 a-4}{5} \text { and } y=\frac{6}{5} \\ & \text { i.e. } & (x, y) & =\left(\frac{3 a-4}{5}, \frac{6}{5}\right)=\text { Focus } \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is the directrix of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>k</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then one of the values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Given, y2=kx-8 

⇒y2=kx-8k

Shifting the origin

Y2=kX where Y=y, X=x-8k

Directrix of standard parabola is X=-k4

Directrix of original parabola is x=8k-k4

Now,  x=1 also coincides with x=8k-k4

On solving, we get k=4,-8
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The parametric equations of the parabola \(y^2-8 x-4 y-12=0\) are
questions.solution:
Given equation of parabola is
\(\begin{aligned}
y^2-8 x-4 y-12 & =0 \\
y^2-4 y & =8 x+12 \\
(y-2)^2 & =8(x+2) \\
4 a & =8 \\
a \quad a & =2
\end{aligned}\)
\(\therefore\) Parametric equations are
\(\begin{aligned}
x+2 & =a t^2, y-2=2 a t \\
x & =-2+2 t^2, y=2+4 t
\end{aligned}\)
\(\therefore\) Hence answer is (c).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The locus of the middle points of chords of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>x</mi></math> drawn through the vertex is a parabola whose
questions.solution:
If the middle point of a chord is α, β, then

α=2t2+02, β=4t+02

Eliminating t, we get α=β22

So, the locus is y2=4x.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Find the equation to the parabola, whose axis parallel to the $y$-axis and which passes through the points $(0,4),(1,9)$ and $(4,5)$ is
questions.solution:
The equation of parabola parallel to $y$-axis is
$y=A x^2+B x+C$ ...(i)
The point $(0,4),(1,9)$ and $(4,5)$ lies on Eq. (i). Then, $\quad 4=0+0+C \Rightarrow C=4$ ...(ii)
$9=A+B+C$
or $\quad 9=A+B+4 \quad(\because C=4)$
$A+B=5$ ...(iii)
and $\quad 5=16 A+4 B+C \quad(\because C=4)$
or $\quad 5=16 A+4 B+4$
$\therefore \quad 16 A+4 B=1$
$4 A+B=\frac{1}{4}$ ...(iv)
Solving Eqs. (iii) and (iv),
we get $\quad A=\frac{-19}{12}, B=\frac{79}{12}$ ...(v)
Substituting the values of $A, B$ and $C$ from Eqs. (ii) and (v) in Eq. (i), then equation of parabola is
$y=\frac{-19}{12} x^2+\frac{79}{12} x+4$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If P is a point on the parabola <math><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>x</mi></math> and A is the point (1, 0), then the locus of the midpoint of the line segment AP is
questions.solution:
α=2x1-1, β=2y1

β2=8α

4y2=4x-2

y2=4x-12

questions.q: If the equation of the tangent at the point <math><mi>P</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>3,4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> on the parabola whose axis is the <math><mi>x</mi></math> -axis is <math><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0,</mn></math> then the distance of the tangent from the focus of the parabola is K, then find 4K
questions.solution:
Slope of the tangent is 34=tan⁡θ
Slope of the focal chord through P3,4 is tan⁡2θ=2tan⁡θ1-tan2θ

i.e. tan2θ=2341-916=247
Equation of the focal chord through P is y-4=247x-3
Focus is S116,0
Distance of the focus from the tangent is
3116-40+732+42=52=2.5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A parabola having its axis parallel to $\mathrm{Y}$-axis, passes through the points $\left(0, \frac{2}{5}\right),(4,-2)$ and $\left(1, \frac{8}{5}\right)$; Then a point that lies on this parabola is
questions.solution:
Since, the axis of the parabola is parallel to y-axis. Then, $(x-h)^2=4 a(y-k)$
Since, $e q^{\mathrm{n}}$ (i) passes through the given points $\left(0, \frac{2}{5}\right)$, $(4,-2) \&\left(1, \frac{8}{5}\right)$
$$
\therefore \mathrm{h}^2=4 \mathrm{a}\left(\frac{2}{5}-\mathrm{k}\right)
$$
$(4-h)^2=4 a(-2-k)$
$$
(1-h)^2=4 a\left(\frac{8}{5}-k\right)
$$
Solving eqn. (ii), (iii) \& (iv), we get
$$
\mathrm{h}=\frac{3}{2}, \mathrm{k}=\frac{7}{4}, \mathrm{a}=-\frac{5}{12}
$$
Putting above values in eqn. (i), we get
$$
\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^2=-\frac{5}{3}\left(y-\frac{7}{4}\right)
$$
Let's check the point $\left(2, \frac{8}{5}\right)$ on equation (v):
$\because \quad \frac{1}{4}=\frac{1}{4} ; \therefore\left(2, \frac{8}{5}\right)$ lies on the parabola.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The focal distance of a point $P$ on the parabola $y^{2}=12 x$ if the ordinate of $P$ is 6, is
questions.solution:
Given parabola is $y^{2}=12 x$
Here $a=3$
For point $P(x, y), y=6$
This point lie on the parabola $\therefore$
$$
(6)^{2}=12 x \Rightarrow x=3
$$
Now, focal distance of point $P$ is $x+a=6$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If $(2,3)$ is the vertex and.' $(3,2)$ is the focus of a parabola, then its equation is
questions.solution: $\because \quad$ Vertex $\mathrm{O} \equiv(2,3)$ focus $\mathrm{S} \equiv(3,2)$ $\because \mathrm{O}$ is the mid point AS $\therefore \frac{\mathrm{x}_1+3}{2}=2$ and $\frac{\mathrm{y}_1+2}{2}=3$ $\Rightarrow x_1=1, y_1=4$ Slope of AS, $m_1=\frac{2-3}{3-2}=-1$ $\because$ Directrix is perpendicular to axis of parabola: $\Rightarrow \mathrm{m}_2=1 \quad\left\{\because \mathrm{m}_2\right.$ is slope of directrix $\}$ $\left(y-y_1\right)=m_2\left(x-x_1\right) \Rightarrow y-4=1(x-1)$ $\Rightarrow \quad y-x=3$ ...(i) Let point $\mathrm{P}$ is on parabola Since, $M P=P S$ $\left\{\because\right.$ as $\mathrm{M}$ is foot of $\perp^{\mathrm{r}}$ from $\mathrm{P}$ to the directrix. $\}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{y-x-3}{\sqrt{1+1}}=\sqrt{(x-3)^2+(y-2)^2} \\ & \Rightarrow(y-x-3)^2=2\left((x-3)^2+(y-2)\right)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-18 x-2 y+2 x y+17=0\end{aligned}$

questions.q: Slope of the chord of parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math> which passes through the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and which subtends an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> at the vertex, is
questions.solution:
Given parabola y2=4ax.

Focus lies at a, 0 and vertex lies at 0, 0.

Now, equation of chord passing through given point is y=m(x+6a)

⇒y-mx6am=1

Homogenizing it with the parabola, y2-4axy-mx6am=0

⇒4amx2+6amy2-4axy=0

It is given that chord subtends 45° angle at origin. Therefore, 

tan⁡45o=24a2-24a2m24am+6am

⇒100a2m2=44a2-24a2m2

⇒49m2=4

⇒m=±27
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
